top_signals.title: Southeast Asian tourism pivots on Iran war as travelers stay local
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Burnham told to launch £100bn tax reform package
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham has been urged to introduce sweeping tax reforms that could generate over £100bn in extra government revenue by a group of influential economists, which includes his own heavyweight adviser Jim O’Neill.  A letter signed by O’Neill, who was formerly a Treasury minister and Goldman Sachs executive, urged the next Prime Minister to introduce [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Shell And ExxonMobil Are Betting Billions On Nigeria's Deepwater Comeback
top_signals.summary: Nigeria is trying to rebuild its energy industry from the ground up, not only trying to recover lost crude production, but working to turn itself into Africa’s dominant refining, gas, capital markets and energy export hub. The clearest test is the Dangote Refinery. The 650,000-barrel-per-day plant has already changed Nigeria’s fuel market and is now preparing for what could become the largest IPO in African history. Dangote Refinery is seeking to raise around $4 billion at a valuation of roughly $40 billion, a listing that would place…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: BonkDAO reports $20M theft from ‘malicious governance proposal’
top_signals.summary: The developers behind the memecoin project said they had informed law enforcement after the attack and were working to “recover funds and identify those responsible.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Canada chooses Germany’s TKMS for landmark submarine contract
top_signals.summary: Joint German-Norwegian team wins multibillion-dollar deal as Ottawa pivots away from US

top_signals.title: Fighting yen psychology is a losing battle
top_signals.summary: Turning the currency around, never easy at the best times, has just become harder given the renewed embrace of the greenback.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Pentagon’s top new weapons programs are 12 years behind schedule: Watchdog
top_signals.summary: A new GAO report examined 104 of the Pentagon’s most expensive weapons programs.

top_signals.title: King Charles just got a lot more treasure
top_signals.summary: Charles' mother Queen Elizabeth II began paying tax only after a public outcry at the government's decision to pay for expensive repairs to Windsor Castle after a fire in 1992.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Luxfer Declares Quarterly Dividend
top_signals.summary: Luxfer Holdings PLC (NYSE: LXFR) (“Luxfer” or the “Company”), a global industrial company innovating niche applications in materials engineering, today announced that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of 13 cents per ordinary share. The dividend will be payable on August 5, 2026 to shareholders of record as of the close of business [...]
